How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-01 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Detects tickers hitting 2+ coherent screeners within one trading day, with validated combos ranked first for amplified edge.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ABVX at score 383 (BREAKOUT, MA_CROSS, SMART_MONEY_SURGE) and AMAT at 347 (BREAKOUT, CLENOW_MOMENTUM, VOLUME_LEADER) lead on raw score, but neither fires a validated combo, which cuts structural edge significantly. CAT at 170 with four screeners - CLENOW_MOMENTUM, PULLBACK, VARIANCE_PREMIUM, VOLUME_LEADER - is the broadest confluence in the batch, though still no matched combo. Score distribution metadata is thin: only n_overlaps=20 is reported with no mean, median, or spread, so calling the batch clustered or top-heavy is not supportable. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 provides no directional tailwind, and zero validated combo matches across all ten names make this a below-average batch by construction quality. AMAT is the first call at low conviction only - large-cap, liquid, clean momentum confirmed by volume, lower binary risk than the small-cap ABVX.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: CAT (score 6, target ~$919.26 (DD20d -9.7%)).

1 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 0 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.
Filter

All Screeners · ranked by backtest quality · type in the search box or click a chip to filter

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, EMA8/21 trend alignment, and low-OI anomaly filtering for signal persistence.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $NLY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1NLY ↑59 Watch$22.20 -0.72%BULLISH SWEEPCALL22$1767KOPENING2567WEAK33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live track shows 100% win rate and 200% average return, but with only 5 resolved trades out of 290 total those numbers are statistically meaningless - the verdict is explicitly flagged as insufficient data, and the monthly cohorts' extreme averages (5527% in May, 3610% in April) reflect options convexity on a handful of winners, not a reliable confirmation of the backtest's 68% win rate and 2.39 Sharpe.

HALF SIZEinsufficient data (5 resolved, need ≥20)

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 290 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is one name: TLT at a score of 60, Watch tier, flagged on a bullish sweep. That score clears no meaningful threshold - n_ge_65 and n_ge_80 are both zero - so this is a marginal signal, not a conviction setup. With only one signal and the screener's own score ceiling sitting at 60 today, the batch is as thin as it gets. The macro regime prints NEUTRAL at 47.53, which neither supports nor kills a long-duration bond trade, but offers no tailwind either. If forced to act, TLT long is the only name available; treat it as low conviction, size accordingly, and wait for a score above 65 before adding.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms the backtest direction cleanly: a 64.6% win rate and a +3.6% average gain against SPY at -0.9% delivers +4.5 points of outperformance, and the May 2026 cohort (WR 61.5%, avg +5.3%) shows no decay, so live results and monthly trends both support rather than contradict the VALID backtest verdict.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NEE leads at 78, the top score in today's batch, fitting Sinclair's utilities sector bias where the structural variance premium runs historically elevated. KMB at 76 and EXC at 75 reinforce the defensive-sector tilt, both Signal-tier names with no named pattern but clean score positioning. The distribution is tightly clustered: 40 of 42 names land at or above 65, the max is 78, and nothing clears 80, which means the batch is decent but uninspiring, no standout conviction trade. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (47.53), neither a tailwind nor a headwind for a short-vol thesis. Take NEE first, Signal tier, light size given the absence of an 80-plus score and the flat macro read.

🧠 Identifies tickers where IV sits in the MID variance risk premium tertile, the zone free of catalyst-pricing distortion and market disbelief.
Construction
Longs tickers in the MID tertile of the variance risk premium (IV vs realized): options priced coherently with realized vol, neither too cheap (LOW = market disbelief) nor too rich (HIGH = catalyst already priced).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

ON and PATH both print 100, the highest possible score, making them the leads in a batch that is top-heavy rather than thin: 26 of 50 names clear 80 and 44 of 50 clear 65, so the screen is generating concentrated signal today, not noise. BBY at 99 is the third name worth noting, adding consumer-discretionary exposure to a list otherwise dominated by tech and growth. Pattern data is null across all picks, so the signal is pure VRP positioning with no secondary confirmation. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (47.53), meaning no regime tailwind but no headwind either. Start with ON at moderate conviction, semiconductor exposure gives it the clearest fundamental anchor alongside the max score.

Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

All eight highlighted names - AMN, AMRZ, APG, ATI, AVAV, BAND, BCYC, CBTX - score 100 at Very strong tier with a LONG direction, but pattern is null across the board, stripping out the one field that would let you rank within the cohort. The score distribution is a ceiling effect, not a signal: 45 of 50 total signals cleared 80 and the median is 100, which means the screener is saturated today and internal differentiation is zero. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 is neither a tailwind nor a drag for this contrarian setup. Without pattern data to separate the names, any pick is arbitrary off this screen alone; treat the batch as a watch list for intraday flow confirmation rather than a ranked conviction list, and do not size up until pattern populates.

Construction
Stocks in established uptrend (price > SMA50 > SMA200) pulling back 5-15% with RSI(14) < 50 and reversal candle. Score blends pullback depth, trend strength, volume contraction, and bb_pctb position.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape broadly confirms the backtest - 59.2% win rate and +3.88% average return beats SPY by 5.4 points - but the tier breakdown is a direct contradiction for today: Very strong names have gone 20% and -5.42% versus Signal names at 59.9% and +3.92%, a gap that makes the entire current batch structurally weaker than what drives the aggregate verdict.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 59% · avg +3.9% · beats SPY -1.6% by +5.4 pts
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 495 resolved at +20d · WR 59% · avg +3.9% · vs SPY -1.6%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 499 closed · WR TB 39% · 15% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

RAMP leads at a perfect 100, followed by ILPT and U both at 95, all firing the LAUNCH pattern within the Very strong tier. The score distribution is abnormally compressed: all 50 signals sit at 80 or above, median 85, which signals either a genuinely rich setup day or a filter that is over-clustering at the top end. The macro regime is NEUTRAL at 47.53, offering no directional lift, which is neither a reason to stand down nor to size up. The critical friction is that the Very strong tier has a 20% win rate and -5.42% average return in 90-day live tracking across only 5 resolved trades, meaning today's entire batch sits in the worst-performing tier by recent data. First preference is RAMP given the maximum score, but conviction is low until the Very strong tier's live record improves meaningfully.

Construction
Flags Stage 4 names (below declining MA30W) with deep 90d drawdown + washed-out RSI as contrarian basket candidates: the Weinstein backtest inverted, Stage 4 entries mean-revert +11.25% SPY-relative at 120d.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

LI at score 91 is the lone Very strong signal and the clear leader in today's batch. AD at 88 is the cleanest runner-up with no names between them and the six-way tie at 84 that includes ACN, BABA, BAH, BSX, BTU, and CTSH. Pattern data is null across every name, which limits single-name surgical conviction and reinforces the basket framing the backtest demands. The distribution is top-heavy: 16 of 28 signals score 80 or above, median sits at 81, and all 28 clear 65, so this is a compressed high-end batch rather than a thin or noisy one. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 47.53, which neither supports nor kills a contrarian mean-reversion setup but warrants sizing discipline. First preference is LI via LEAPS at moderate conviction, treated as the basket anchor, with AD as the secondary leg.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Detects vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile and VIX term structure to flag sell-vol entries when fear peaks.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑61 Watch$745.76 -0.14%SELL VOL1.33
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (WR 62.5%, avg +1.46% vs SPY +1.42%, verdict KEEP) barely confirms an edge - the key number is the tier split: Signal-tier trades (n=7) run 85.7% WR and +2.73% avg while Watch-tier (n=17) sits at 52.9% and +0.94%, meaning the live record is almost entirely carried by a handful of high-score signals that today's batch does not include, and April's 100% WR on just 4 trades flatters the monthly cohort trend against May's more honest 61% on 18 trades.

🧠 Flags options volume 5x above 20-day mean on calm, non-extended tickers to surface smart-money positioning before a directional move.
Construction
Stocks with options volume > 5× mean(20d) + premium > $50K + Vol/OI > 5. V3 filters now applied at write time: keeps only calm (vol<1.5× avg) and non-extended (±5% from EMA20) tickers, drops LATE-timing chasing signals.
Top picks: $TLT $QQQ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikeDTEPremiumVol/OIIVDeltaTimingFlowOIToxicity
1TLT ↑64 Watch$85.52 -1.04%BEARISH SWEEPPUT87.57$261K42.10EARLY310.80
2QQQ ↑50 Watch$725.17 -1.52%BULLISH SWEEPCALL7286$2410K4.2016.5%0.55EARLY5x REPEAT7690.93
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape reports 100% win rate across 23 resolved signals with avg +189.2% against SPY at -0.6%, a result that hard-contradicts the backtest's 61% win rate, but 23 resolutions out of 620 total signals is a 3.7% resolution rate, far too thin to override the backtest's MARGINAL verdict or its p-value of 0.097 that already fails the 0.05 threshold.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NLY leads today at a score of 85, the only name clearing the 80 threshold, with a bullish sweep on a mREIT in a rate-sensitive tape worth watching. IWM prints 77 but the pattern is BEARISH SWEEP against a LONG direction label, a direct conflict that kills actionable conviction there. QQQ at 70 with a clean bullish sweep is the next coherent read, though a NEUTRAL macro regime (47.53) provides no directional fuel for index longs. The score distribution is thin: 1 of 12 signals above 80, only 3 above 65, median sitting at 56, which makes today a weak batch by this screener's own standards. Take NLY first at low-to-moderate conviction, skip IWM until the direction-pattern conflict resolves.

🧠 Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs by strength, acceleration, and RS crossover vs SPY to identify sectors entering or exiting momentum phases.
Construction
Classifies 11 GICS sector ETFs into 8 categories: EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, LATE_CHASE, EXHAUSTED, DECELERATING, NEW_DOWN, DEAD, NEUTRAL. Uses strength + acceleration + RS crossover vs SPY.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

XLC and XLE share the top score of 80 and Strong tier, making them the only names worth acting on today. XLV at 65 is the sole Signal-tier name below that pair, a notch down in conviction. The score distribution is top-heavy and then drops sharply: two names at 80, one at 65, one at 60, and four clustered at 50 Watch, which is a thin upper tier sitting above a wide mass of fence-sitters. Pattern fields are null across the board, so the GICS acceleration category (EARLY_ENTRY, RIDING, etc.) is unresolved for every pick, which limits position sizing logic. Macro regime scores NEUTRAL at 47.53, neither a tailwind nor a headwind. Preference is XLC first at modest conviction, given the null pattern data and a macro setup that does not amplify sector momentum trades.

🧠 Identifies stocks down 40%-plus from their 52-week high showing capitulation-volume spikes and reversal candles, targeting mean reversion as exhausted sellers give way.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day win rate of 45.7% contradicts the backtest's 63.3%, a real gap, but the Very Strong tier (n=5) recovers credibility with a 60% win rate and a +15.2% average gain, and monthly cohorts confirm a directional improvement from January's brutal -9.5% average through May's +2.8%, suggesting the setup is healing rather than broken.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is numerically crowded at the top: 49 of 50 signals scored 80 or above, median at 86, which makes the distribution tightly clustered rather than discriminating. LDOS, ODV, and ORLA all print perfect 100s at Very Strong tier with BREAKOUT patterns - three simultaneous perfect scores is the highest-quality sub-set the screener can produce, and they are the starting point. INTR, UUUU, and WW at 95 add a second row of Very Strong names, giving the batch unusual depth at the top end. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 neither supports nor suppresses the mean-reversion thesis, so position sizing should stay conservative rather than aggressive. Lead with LDOS at first priority, high conviction, given the perfect score and Very Strong tier; treat ODV and ORLA as equal alternates if capital allows.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Ranks US equities by annualized exponential-regression slope times R-squared over 90 days, isolating stocks with smooth, persistent uptrends above their 100-day moving average.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

The macro regime at NEUTRAL 47.53 is not a veto but it is not a tailwind either, making this a name-selection exercise rather than a tape-wide trade. Pass on both today unless a name-specific catalyst thesis overrides the screener entirely.

🧠 Detects simultaneous 2-sigma intermarket correlation breaks across major pairs and fires a contrarian SPY long, scaling severity with break count.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Data confidence HALF SIZE doctrine 8-step: MARGINAL at h=5d (Sharpe 0.5-0.6), REJECT at h>=10d; 60d academic drift not reproduced Full methodology →
🧠 Detects short-term price continuation after a positive EPS surprise by scoring gap size, gap hold, surprise magnitude, and analyst revision activity.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.
Top picks: $BB
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySurpriseConsistencyPost-earn ret
1BB ↑95●●● Very strong$12.81 +1.26%50.0%420.7%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape at WR 61% and avg +4.3% against SPY at -0.1% confirms a real short-hold edge despite the backtest's marginal rating, with the May 2026 cohort at WR 59.8% across 709 trades reinforcing that verdict - April's six-name cohort at 50% WR is too thin to read either way.

Today's batch is a singleton - BB at score 95, Very strong tier, PRIMED pattern, the only name the screener surfaced. With one signal and a median that equals the max at 95, there is no score distribution to characterize - this is a thin batch by any standard. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (47.53), imposing no directional headwind but providing no tailwind either. In 90-day tracking, the Very strong tier posts WR 60.6% and avg 4.1%, which actually trails the Signal and Watch tier averages, so the tier label alone does not sharpen the edge on BB. Take BB first and only, at low-to-moderate conviction - one name in a neutral tape is not a moment to size aggressively.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All 50 signals score exactly 100 with zero dispersion - median 100, every name at or above the 80 threshold - so the screener offers no internal ranking today. Among the top picks, CRWV and BWXT are the names most worth examining at Very strong tier and score 100, though neither carries a reported pattern, leaving the cross itself as the sole entry rationale. The score distribution is fully saturated and tells you nothing about relative strength within the batch. The NEUTRAL macro regime (score 47.53) is an indifferent backdrop that neither confirms nor rejects a momentum read, and the live track record argues against acting aggressively here. If forced to a preference, BWXT is first given sector characteristics, but conviction is low - treat this as a watchlist exercise, not a trigger.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape supports a KEEP verdict with a 56.5% win rate and +4.0% average return beating SPY by 6.0 points, but that aggregate masks a damaging tier inversion: the Watch tier (59.3% WR, +5.0% avg) beats the Very strong tier (45.8% WR, -2.5% avg), meaning the screener's highest-confidence signals are its worst performers over the recent window.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACHC leads at a perfect 100 score with BEAM and BV both at 95, all carrying the PRIMED pattern, but every name in the top tier today lands in 'Very strong', which is the single worst-performing tier in the 90-day tape at a 45.8% win rate and -2.5% average return. The score distribution is broad at the base but top-heavy, with all 50 signals clearing 65 and 17 clearing 80, suggesting the screen is not being selective enough today. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 provides no meaningful tailwind or headwind for squeeze momentum. First preference goes to ABEO or ABX at the Strong tier, where the historical return profile is materially better, sized at low conviction given the tier inversion problem.

🧠 Ranks RUT-1000 stocks by relative volume against 10/30/90-day averages multiplied by 1/5/20/90-day price momentum, surfacing names where volume confirms trend acceleration.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

AIG, META, WING, and KLAC all print perfect 100 scores and AMAT comes in at 99, but every name in today's batch sits in the Very strong tier with zero pattern data attached, so there is no structural differentiator beyond raw score. The score distribution is fully compressed: all 50 signals score at or above 80 with a median of 96, meaning the screener is not ranking today, it is printing a ceiling. Macro is NEUTRAL at 47.53, which neither lifts nor blocks the trade. The fatal problem is that the Very strong tier is the worst live performer in the book at a 41.5% win rate and negative 4.09% average 20-day return, so every name the screener is flagging as its best sits in its empirically worst cohort. If forced to pick one name for liquidity and options depth, META at 100 is the least objectionable, but conviction here is minimal.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape contradicts the unadjusted backtest entirely: WR of 41.4% and avg return of -5.0% against SPY's -2.0% earns a KILL verdict, and the key number is the tier inversion where Very Strong names average -8.6%, making score rank an unreliable size guide in the current environment. The April and May 2026 monthly cohorts show WR near 52% and positive average returns, which contradicts the 90-day verdict and likely reflects a different measurement window or methodology, not a recoverable edge.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CWAN at a score of 93 is the lone Very Strong name and the clearest signal in today's batch, but the live tier data is a warning: Very Strong names have posted the worst realized performance of any tier over 90 days, so the top score here does not map to top conviction. GGR (84) and AIFU (83) are the next names worth watching, though no pattern data is available for any pick in today's batch, which limits differentiation beyond the raw score. The distribution is moderately top-heavy: 7 names score 80 or above against a median of 67 across 37 signals, so there is a real cluster at the high end but the middle of the book is thin. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 offers no directional tailwind. Given the tier inversion in live results, where Watch-tier names (WR 47.2%, avg -2.2%) have outperformed Very Strong names (WR 35.7%, avg -8.6%), PRA at 78 is the marginal first-look preference, and only at low conviction.

Data confidence STARTER backtest_dalton_excess 2026-04 (500 tickers 2022-2026): raw excess = coin flip (WR 51%); WITH extreme-location + tail>=1.8xATR filter +3.18% @10d, N=74 only, no block bootstrap Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional price rejection at Market Profile value-area extremes using rejection-tail bars at least 1.8x ATR, signaling mean reversion back toward value.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.
Top picks: $GLRE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %Kerberos trap
1GLRE ↑100●●● Very strong$16.36 +1.11%LOW2.0928.5%·
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Three resolved trades (66.7% win rate, +3.85% avg at 20 days versus a SPY baseline of 0.06%) look directionally consistent with the filtered backtest result of +3.18%, but N=3 against a minimum threshold of 20 means the live data contradicts nothing and confirms nothing, making the key number here 3, not 66.7%.

🧠 Catches post-earnings drift entries via a weekly red-candle pullback resolved by a close above the prior week's high within four weeks of an earnings gap-up.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.
Top picks: $APD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayGapVol ratioBars ago
1APD ↑100●●● Very strong$306.40 +4.51%9.1%2.401
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Opening gap on one of the last 3 bars: 5.0% ≤ gap < 10.0%
  • Volume confirmation: gap-day volume / 20d average > 1.5
  • Dollar-volume ≥ $50M
Score
gap_pct × 15 (not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled, top pick = 100). LONG.

Today's batch is a single name: APD scores 100 and lands in the Very strong tier with no pattern detail available, which limits the read immediately. APD is the default and only first look; conviction is moderate given the absence of pattern context, the one-name screen, and a regime that is neither constructive nor hostile.

Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape directly contradicts any optimism from the backtest: 90-day resolved WR of 46% and avg return of -0.5% earns a KILL verdict, and while the Strong tier shows a legitimately good 57.8% WR and +15.3% avg, the Watch tier (139 trades, WR 36.7%, avg -10.6%) is destroying the composite - the key number is that 46% overall win rate against a backtest Sharpe of 1.53 built on only 55 trades with a p-Bonferroni of 1.0, confirming the backtest was never trustworthy.

What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ALIT (score 100, Very strong) and AGL (score 94, Very strong) are the only names worth serious attention - both clear the tier threshold with maximum or near-maximum scores on breakout strength and volume, though pattern fields are null for every pick, which is a data gap. INTW at 76 (Signal tier) is borderline and the remaining five names are Watch-tier noise scoring 43 or below. The score distribution is extremely top-heavy: median sits at 25 across 37 signals, with only 2 names at or above 80, so the batch is thin quality. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 47.53, offering no real tailwind but not a hard veto either. First preference is ALIT over AGL, but conviction is low given the screener carries a live KILL verdict - size accordingly or wait for the tape to confirm.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape shows 51.3% WR and +1.8% avg beating SPY by 3.5 points, and May, April, and March 2026 monthly cohorts all print positive avg returns, confirming the aggregate short edge is real, but the by-tier split (Strong tier: 30.8% WR, -11.7% avg) is the key number and it directly contradicts using today's top-scoring names.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ALIT leads at score 90 in the Very strong tier on a BREAKDOWN pattern, but the 90-day by-tier breakdown is a direct warning: Very strong carries a 0% win rate and -7.6% avg, meaning the highest-conviction shorts in this screener have historically destroyed the trader, not the stock. APPS and MQ both score 85 in the Strong tier on TRIGGER patterns, and Strong is nearly as toxic at 30.8% WR and -11.7% avg. The score distribution is top-heavy but misleading: 43 of 50 signals cluster at or above 65 with only 6 reaching 80, and a median of 65 puts most of the batch at the low end of Signal territory. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (47.53), offering no directional tailwind to sharpen a short hedge. Given the tier-inversion and a backtest verdict of REJECT, the best-odds entry is ABCL or ABSI at score 75 in the Signal tier, where historical avg is positive at the aggregate level, and conviction should remain low.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is entirely degenerate: all 50 signals print at median 99, every name scores 100 or near it, and the ceiling is hard at 100. That compression makes rank differentiation meaningless today. The top names are a dense semiconductor and storage cluster - STX, WDC, DELL on the hardware side, INTC and AMD on logic, LRCX and AMAT in equipment, FLEX in EMS - all scoring 100 with no pattern data attached. A macro regime score of 47.53 (NEUTRAL) does not actively favor or penalize this kind of cyclical momentum cluster, but it offers no tailwind either. Given the backtest verdict is REJECT (alpha -0.8%/yr, p=0.25, the +467% headline was a leveraged-ETF artifact), no position sizing is warranted; if forced to track one name from this batch, LRCX earns a first look on equipment cycle positioning, at low conviction only.

🧠 Flags weekly RSI(14) below 30 then confirms entry via daily RSI crossover, candlestick reversal, and demethod zone scoring to trade oversold mean-reversion in US equities.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $DDL $DKL $GDS

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape confirms the KILL verdict rather than contradicting it: 90-day average return of +1.5% trails SPY by 130 basis points, and the monthly cohorts show a deteriorating trend from +6.19% avg in March to -0.44% avg in May, meaning the edge is evaporating in real time, not just in backtest rejection.

DKL is the only name worth attention, scoring 66 as the sole Signal-tier pick and the sole name above the 65 threshold across 26 signals today. BORR (63) and FANG (61) are Watch-tier SETUPs but sit in the middle of a compressed range where max is 66 and median is 52, with zero names reaching 80 - this batch has no high-conviction entries. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 provides no directional lift, meaning the screener is leaning on weak technicals without a macro tailwind to assist. DKL goes first if you touch anything, but position sizing should reflect the thin score ceiling, not a full-risk SETUP.

🧠 Detects unusual volume, premium, and OI delta on crypto-linked ETFs to front-run spot crypto momentum, with no validated post-2024 edge.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

3FBTC ↑55 Watch$52.23 +2.31%45.08-0.0%risk_on
4IBIT ↑55 Watch$34.00 +2.13%45.050.0%risk_on

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tape contradicts any residual case for this screener: the 90-day KILL verdict holds, with Signal-tier signals averaging -20.2% over 20 days on 8 resolved trades and the sole Strong-tier resolution down 13.2%, while the backtest returned zero survivors across 16 adversarial tests post-spot-ETF 2024.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SMIN at 74 and IFRA at 73 sit atop the batch and are the only names worth examining first, though neither carries a pattern flag, which removes one layer of conviction. PPA at 70 adds a defense-theme angle that is at least regime-coherent given ongoing budget tailwinds, but again no pattern. The score distribution is compressed and soft: max is 74, median is 60, zero names clear 80, and only 10 of 50 signals reach 65, which is a thin and uninspiring spread. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 does not argue for pressing theme exposure, and this batch reflects that flatness. If forced to act, SMIN gets first look over IFRA given the score edge, but conviction is low and position sizing should reflect that.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names print at score 100, all Very strong tier, all LONG: BL, CELH, HDV, MAZE, QDEL, RNA, SRTY, and TNDM. Pattern data is null across the board, so there is no structural confirmation layered on top of the score. SRTY is a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF flagging LONG inside a strength screener, which sits uneasily alongside the other names and warrants a hard look before touching it. Score distribution is top-heavy: 8 of 50 signals at the maximum of 100, median at 70, and 41 of 50 at or above 65, meaning the batch skews compressed toward the upper bound rather than spreading across the range. Macro is NEUTRAL at 47.53, no regime tailwind, and live tracking shows Very strong tier produces the weakest avg return at 3.43% versus 9.54% for Watch, so overall conviction is low - if forced to a first name, CELH on known momentum characteristics, but size thin until pattern data populates.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ASML at 97 and FB at 93 are the only two names in 'Very strong' territory and the clear standouts in an otherwise middling batch. No pattern data is attached to any pick, so the signal rests entirely on position-change scoring with no technical confirmation. The distribution is thin at the top and clustered near the median: only 2 of 50 signals clear 80, median sits at 55, which reads as a weak batch, not a high-conviction sweep. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 adds no directional tailwind. ASML is the first look at the highest score, but flag that all 13F data is 60 to 90 days stale by construction, so treat any position as already priced or partially moved.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

SVRE and JEF both score 100 today, the highest possible, Very strong tier, with no additional pattern data filed alongside either name. PBLS and DPC follow at 99, also Very strong, also pattern-null, and COE and SMMT sit at 98 rounding out the top of a tight cluster. The score distribution is severely compressed - all 44 signals land at or above 80 and the median is 94, which means the screener is not separating names at the margin in any useful way today. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 47.53 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for insider-buy signals specifically, so there is no macro lift to lean on. With pattern fields null across the board, JEF is the first preference at low-to-moderate conviction given the round 100 score and recognizable institutional name, but the lack of differentiation across 44 bunched signals argues for small initial sizing.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks non-financial, non-utility US equities by EV divided by EBIT ascending, surfacing the statistically cheapest operating businesses by enterprise value.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).

🧠 AI read · today's batch

BZ scores a perfect 100, making it the cheapest name in today's universe on an EV/EBIT basis, followed closely by WRD at 97 and KROS at 93, all three landing in the Very Strong tier. No pattern data exists for any pick, so the signal is purely valuation rank with no momentum or technical overlay. The score distribution is moderately top-heavy: 7 of 16 names clear 80 and 11 clear 65, with a median of 73, suggesting a decent spread rather than a compressed cluster. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (47.53), which neither accelerates nor blocks a deep-value thesis, but gives no cyclical tailwind. First preference is BZ on score alone, at low conviction given zero backtest validation.

🧠 Detects coordinated insider conviction via SEC Form 4 cluster buys, weighted by rank and dollar size, on the premise that C-suite agreement signals private fundamental confidence.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live record - 59% win rate, avg +4.0% at 20 days versus SPY +0.4%, verdict KEEP - is directionally encouraging, but the April 2026 cohort cooling to 44% WR and +1.2% avg after a strong May (62% WR, +4.7%) flags that the edge is regime-sensitive and not locked in, a pattern the missing backtest cannot yet quantify.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch of 11 signals is undifferentiated: every name scores exactly 60, the ceiling for this screener, with no ticker clearing 65 and zero clearing 80, so rank-ordering within the batch is meaningless. ADSK and FISV are the most recognizable names given their market cap and liquidity, making them the lowest-friction entries if the cluster signal holds up under manual Form 4 review. KMX is worth a second look given the consumer-discretionary macro sensitivity and any insider conviction there carries a contrarian tilt. Pattern data is null across the board, which strips out one layer of confirmation. The macro regime sits at NEUTRAL (47.53), neither a tailwind nor a headwind, and a flat-score batch in a flat-regime day is not the spot to press hard. If forced to pick one, ADSK first at low conviction, and only after verifying the Form 4 filings directly.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=17 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Scores retail stocks on social-attention z-score, call/put skew, and price stretch, then fades short when no earnings catalyst exists.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.
Top picks: $BXP $CF $ARE
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTierCall vol/OIChg
1BXP ↓100●●● Very strong$67.76 +2.19%FOMO1.01-0.2%
2CF ↓100●●● Very strong$108.16 -0.09%FOMO2.310.5%
3ARE ↓84●● Strong$53.01 +0.30%EUPHORIA0.840.5%
4ACN ↓75● Signal$131.13 +5.38%GREED0.751.9%
5BKNG ↓75● Signal$182.64 +2.47%GREED0.751.2%
6BB ↓74● Signal$12.81 +1.26%GREED0.742.1%
7AMD ↓68● Signal$540.88 -6.89%GREED0.68-4.7%
8CELH ↓65● Signal$31.87 +8.85%GREED0.653.5%
9B ↓51 Watch$36.45 -0.76%GREED0.51-0.2%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)
  • Tiers: ≥1.00 FOMO, ≥0.75 EUPHORIA, ≥0.50 GREED (scan threshold 0.50)
  • Filters: price > $10, options volume > 1000, total OI > 4000
Score
clamp(0,100, call_vol_oi_ratio × 100). SHORT (fade the retail crowd).

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs by Antonacci dual momentum, requiring positive 12-month absolute return and top relative rank versus peers.
Construction
Antonacci dual momentum on a macro ETF set: absolute (12m return > 0) AND relative (top rank vs peers). Long the leaders.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 Flags individual stocks lagging their sector move by scoring sector relative strength against stock under-performance to surface catch-up candidates.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

BSX and ZTS are the most institutionally relevant names in the set, but both sit at Watch/50 with null patterns, offering no technical trigger to act on. No name earns first-look status here; the entire batch is bench until scores disperse and at least one pattern populates.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies top relative-strength tickers inside bullish sectors, scored on 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and intra-cluster rank.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $AAT $AAMI $ABCL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1AAT ↑82●● Strong$25.22 +2.15%Real Estate19.0%31.5%
2AAMI ↑76● Signal$71.98 +0.64%Financials9.2%52.1%
3ABCL ↑74● Signal$7.60 -3.18%Healthcare7.8%47.3%
4A ↑65● Signal$133.39 +0.42%Healthcare15.9%17.1%
5ABCB ↑56 Watch$91.65 +1.54%Financials4.9%15.6%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live tape (55.4% WR, +4.3% avg, beating SPY -1.8% by 6.1 points, KEEP verdict) is the only statistical foundation here, since the formal backtest remains incomplete at N=0, and the single monthly cohort (May 2026, 55.7% WR, +10.8% avg) is directionally consistent but too thin to constitute a trend - the key number is that +6.1 pt SPY spread, which is the sole reason this screener stays on the dashboard.

Today's batch is thin: five signals, median score 74, only AAT clearing 80. AAT leads at 82 but sits in the Strong tier, which in live tracking runs at 47.8% WR and only +1.9% avg, the worst risk-adjusted tier in the set. AAMI (76, Signal) and ABCL (74, Signal) are the more interesting names because the Signal tier is the screener's live edge, posting 72% WR and +9.2% avg on 54 resolved trades. Macro is NEUTRAL at 47.53, providing neither a sector tailwind nor a headwind, so this is purely a stock-specific call. Start with AAMI at medium conviction, not AAT, because tier beats raw score in this system's live record.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put minus call skew spikes versus a 90-day baseline, reading extreme hedging demand as a contrarian signal when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $MU $MSTR $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1MU ↑68● Signal$1032.28 -10.57%102%60—BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
2MSTR ↑66● Signal$93.39 +7.43%66%950.03BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
3AVGO ↑51 Watch$369.34 -2.23%47%430.01BUY PREMIUM (IV cheap)
4META ↑50 Watch$612.91 +8.81%38%77-0.08BUY CALL (call skew che…
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape posts WR 54% and avg +9.1% beating SPY by 7.8 points, which supports the KEEP verdict, but the tier breakdown directly contradicts any confidence in today's signal - Very strong and Strong both run negative average returns (-5.1% and -4.6%) while Watch and Signal at lower scores drive all the alpha, meaning the aggregate WR flatters a screener whose highest-conviction tier is its most dangerous.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags pre-earnings names where straddle implied vol underprices the Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, targeting vol expansion into the event.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $JPM $GE $WFC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1JPM ↑75● Signal$334.07 +2.06%PRE_IV_RAMP12—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2GE ↑65● Signal$374.94 +0.32%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3WFC ↑65● Signal$85.94 +3.99%PRE_IV_RAMP12—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).

If you touch JPM here, treat it as a low-conviction probe only, size accordingly, and wait for the signal count and score quality to improve before committing real capital.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breakouts above Value Area High that close back inside the value area, trapping buyers and generating a short signal.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $ALGM $AMDL $ALHC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1ALGM ↓60 Watch$63.20 -9.22%BULL_TRAP69.0558.45
2AMDL ↓60 Watch$71.42 -13.69%BULL_TRAP79.9033.90
3ALHC ↓55 Watch$24.01 +0.84%BULL_TRAP24.3818.27
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breaks below Value Area Low or above VAH that reverse next session, trapping short or long momentum players for a mean-reversion move.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.

#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags stocks where put IV crushes call IV at above-90th-percentile extremes, identifying concentrated directional hedging or speculative put demand.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $AVGO
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1AVGO ↑100●●● Very strong$369.34 -2.23%16.7468.7%97BEAR_SKEW_EXTREME
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape on 8 resolved trades shows a 62.5% win rate but a negative average return of -3.14% against SPY at +0.98%, and the tier split is the only number that matters: Very strong names averaged +19.3% at 100% win rate while the two Strong-tier signals averaged -55.8% at 0%, meaning tier filtering is everything and the WATCH verdict is exactly right given the sample is too thin at 8 to call the edge real.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 minus HV20 z-score exceeds 1.5 sigma on a 90-day window, identifying outsized fear premium candidates for short-volatility trades.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $NFLX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1NFLX ↓62 Watch$74.19 +3.91%47%30%1.55FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 With no valid backtest to compare against (N=25 resolved trades, below the 30-trade threshold), the live 90-day tape stands alone at WR 44% and avg +2.0% versus SPY +1.2%, a thin 80-basis-point edge undercut by the tier breakdown showing the 'Strong' bucket printing a negative avg of -4.8% on 29 trades and the May 2026 cohort collapsing to -0.22% avg after April's +10.8%, confirming the edge is lumpy and regime-dependent rather than structural.

🧠 Detects options mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and institutional flow, seeking volatility premium edge.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $VXX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1VXX ↓86●● Strong$22.32 +1.04%49%261%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

Altman

EOD
Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using Altman Z-score distress signals to surface financial stress or recovery inflection points.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is empty. One signal fired and scored zero, clearing neither the 65 nor the 80 threshold. There are no top picks to evaluate. The score distribution is not clustered or thin, it is flatlined. Macro regime sits at NEUTRAL with a 47.53 score, which offers no tailwind to override a dead screen. No actionable name exists today.

🧠 STFR screens US equities via end-of-day logic defined in its source script; the exact construction and edge mechanism are not documented in this payload.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: price > $10 (shortable)
  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 VSA flags institutional supply and demand imbalances through price-spread and volume relationships to identify accumulation or distribution turning points.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

No actionable preference exists from this run, pass on the screener today.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
